what_it_does: Maintains a reorganisation blueprint and progress ledger for counterpart/design, covering project principles, territories, proof status, drift issues, graph and merge-seam notes, workstreams, staged migration, target tree, path rewrites, verification gates, and open decisions.
observations: The file begins with a live progress log that records completed reorganisation slices, commits, proof results, render-by-use checks, and remaining ownership notes.
It defines six first-class territories: registry, dna, surface, app, server, and pieces, plus supporting territories.
It records core operating laws such as derive-never-place, registry-is-truth, equal-and-opposite typed edges, and dispatch by territory.
It includes an older proof-suite snapshot that says rebuild is red, while the progress log later says the reorg is closed and proofs are green.
It lists a corrected drift table with 22 issues, including proof failures, manifest honesty, substrate authority, role-layer gaps, graph support, and cross-repo merge-seam representation.
It adds a dedicated section for the graph instrument and the DNA-to-composition merge seam.
It lays out workstreams A through E, a staged build sequence, a target directory structure, an atomic path-rewrite map, and per-stage verification expectations.
The visible file ends in a truncated open decision section.
standouts: The progress log is detailed enough to function as a migration audit trail with commit hashes and verification notes.
The document treats directory layout, proof commands, generated artifacts, and design principles as one system map.
The graph instrument and icon/token merge seam are described as structural concerns that the reorganisation must preserve.
The file keeps both future-state architecture and historical execution notes in the same document.
conventions: Uses numbered sections, stage labels, workstream letters, tables, fenced trees, inline paths, commit hashes, and status phrases such as DONE, VERIFIED BY USE, and REMAINING.
Uses strong project vocabulary around DNA, substrate, registry, surface, pieces, proofs, seams, and territory.
Keeps path references in backticks and records exact migration paths using old-path to new-path notation.
Appends progress above older planning material, so landed work and earlier intended work coexist in the same file.
concerns: The live progress log says the reorg is closed, while later sections still describe remaining Stage 2, E.3b, E.3c, and red proof-suite states.
Some sections appear stale relative to the progress log, including the current proof-suite status, staged sequence, target tree, and path-rewrite map.
The progress log is long and mixes ownership notes, completed work, deferred follow-ups, and historical classification data before the orientation section.
The visible open-decisions section is truncated mid-sentence.
notes: Brief someone on this file as a living architecture-and-migration ledger whose top log is newer than several retained planning sections.
questions: Which sections are intended to remain operative after the progress log marks the reorg closed?
Should older red-status proof tables be retained as history or revised to match the close-out state?
Is the truncated open-decisions section complete in the actual file?
Are repeated REMAINING blocks meant to describe different moments in time or the same unresolved set?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose mostly matches: it is a living map and staged build path, but it also functions as a dense historical progress ledger with some internally stale planning material retained.
apparent_themes: Design DNA as both visual and verbal language
intention_signals: It seems to be trying to keep a complex repository reorganisation aligned with design-system principles, proof gates, ownership boundaries, and path-migration details while preserving context for future work.
novelty: A repository reorganisation plan is written as a design-system substrate ledger, combining filesystem migration, proof status, render checks, design laws, and cross-repo seam notes in one document.
contribution: It contributes the interpretive map of counterpart-design's territories, migration rationale, proof gates, structural issues, and design laws that a fused system would keep as planning and governance context.
summary_for_embedding: This is a living reorganisation blueprint and progress ledger for counterpart-design that records DNA-system principles, territory structure, proof status, drift issues, graph and merge-seam notes, staged migrations, path rewrites, verification gates, and open decisions.
intention_for_embedding: It was written to guide and audit the repository's move from a mixed tree into named design-system territories while preserving proofability, addressability, and live-surface work.
